title: Investors Hung Their Hats on Peloton and Zoom Last Year. What Now?
content: Some “stay-at-home” stocks that were pandemic-era darlings have experienced brutal sell-offs.
en_title: Investors Hung Their Hats on Peloton and Zoom Last Year. What Now?
en_content: Some “stay-at-home” stocks that were pandemic-era darlings have experienced brutal sell-offs.

title: A group of Activision workers say their C.E.O. should step down.
content: More than a hundred employees protested at the company’s studio in Irvine, Calif., on Tuesday.
en_title: A group of Activision workers say their C.E.O. should step down.

title: The Tech Gifts That Are Hard to Buy This Holiday Season
content: The list of scarce gadgets is long, driven by a chip shortage. Don’t wait until Black Friday for the best deals.

title: L.A.’s Staples Center will be renamed Crypto.com Arena.
content: The rebranding, which will take effect on Christmas Day, is the latest example of a company at the center of a technology boom paying to rename a well-known sports arena.
en_title: L.A.’s Staples Center will be renamed Crypto.com Arena.
en_content: The rebranding, which will take effect on Christmas Day, is the latest example of a company at the center of a technology boom paying to rename a well-known sports arena.

title: Uneasy About the Economy, Americans Are Spending Anyway
content: Retail sales jumped 1.7 percent in October, the third monthly increase, a rise that highlighted the resilience of the U.S. economy.

more_info.byline: By Ben Casselman, Sapna Maheshwari and Coral Murphy Marcos

en_title: Uneasy About the Economy, Americans Are Spending Anyway
en_content: Retail sales jumped 1.7 percent in October, the third monthly increase, a rise that highlighted the resilience of the U.S. economy.

title: The Worst of Both Worlds: Zooming From the Office
content: Work life for many is in a mushy middle ground, and what’s at stake isn’t just who is getting talked over in meetings. It’s whether flexibility is sustainable, even with all the benefits it confers.
en_title: The Worst of Both Worlds: Zooming From the Office
en_content: Work life for many is in a mushy middle ground, and what’s at stake isn’t just who is getting talked over in meetings. It’s whether flexibility is sustainable, even with all the benefits it confers.

title: Energy-Efficient Isn’t Enough, So Homes Go ‘Net Zero’
content: Demand for residences that produce as much energy as they consume is being spurred by climate concerns, consumer appetite and more affordable solar technology.
en_title: Energy-Efficient Isn’t Enough, So Homes Go ‘Net Zero’
en_content: Demand for residences that produce as much energy as they consume is being spurred by climate concerns, consumer appetite and more affordable solar technology.

title: Will an Island in Indonesia Become a New Frontier in the Space Race?
content: An Indigenous clan fears it will lose its place in the world as the government pursues a quest to open a spaceport and lure the billionaire SpaceX founder Elon Musk.

more_info.byline: By Dera Menra Sijabat, Richard C. Paddock and Ulet Ifansasti

en_title: Will an Island in Indonesia Become a New Frontier in the Space Race?
en_content: An Indigenous clan fears it will lose its place in the world as the government pursues a quest to open a spaceport and lure the billionaire SpaceX founder Elon Musk.

title: Pandemic Stocks Have Become Passé
content: Peloton, Zoom and others make way for the shares of “reopening” companies.

title: Inflation in Britain climbs to a 10-year high.
content: Prices rose 4.2 percent last month from a year earlier, more than double the Bank of England’s target.

en_content: Prices rose 4.2 percent last month from a year earlier, more than double the Bank of England’s target.

title: Travel Industry Takes Crucial First Step Toward Combating Climate Change
content: More than 300 travel companies, tourism boards and countries have signed the Glasgow Declaration on Climate Action in Tourism, the first step for a shared road map to cut carbon emissions.

des_facet: United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change

title: The prosecution’s case against Elizabeth Holmes, the Theranos founder.
content: Jurors are being asked to decide whether Ms. Holmes misled investors by withholding crucial information or whether investors simply failed to do their due diligence.
en_title: The prosecution’s case against Elizabeth Holmes, the Theranos founder.
en_content: Jurors are being asked to decide whether Ms. Holmes misled investors by withholding crucial information or whether investors simply failed to do their due diligence.

title: Victims of Sexual Misconduct Testify Against Forced Arbitration
content: In emotional testimony before the House Judiciary Committee, four survivors of sexual misconduct said they had been required to stay silent because of “forced arbitration” clauses in their contracts.

title: F.D.A. Plans to Authorize Pfizer Boosters for All Adults This Week
content: Regulators are set to clear extra doses of Pfizer-BioNTech’s vaccine as soon as Thursday, after states began expanding eligibility on their own.

en_title: F.D.A. Plans to Authorize Pfizer Boosters for All Adults This Week

title: U.S. to Buy Enough of Pfizer’s Covid Antiviral Pills for 10 Million People
content: The antiviral drugs have helped inspire hope among senior administration officials that the United States will be able to curb the devastating toll from the Delta variant.

content: He was a groundbreaking master of the visual pun, part of a generation of designers for whom the aesthetic was in service to the idea.

title: The ‘Fearless Girl’ Statue Is in Limbo
content: Its permit expires on Nov. 29, but the statue famous for staring down the New York Stock Exchange won’t get a city hearing on a more permanent home until December, at the earliest.
en_title: The ‘Fearless Girl’ Statue Is in Limbo

title: JPMorgan says Tesla owes it $162 million because of an Elon Musk tweet.
content: The 2018 tweet, in which Mr. Musk claimed he had secured funding to take Tesla private, initially sent the automaker’s shares soaring. But they sank when it became clear that no such deal had been reached.
en_title: JPMorgan says Tesla owes it $162 million because of an Elon Musk tweet.
en_content: The 2018 tweet, in which Mr. Musk claimed he had secured funding to take Tesla private, initially sent the automaker’s shares soaring. But they sank when it became clear that no such deal had been reached.

title: Amazon agrees to pay $500,000 in California over claims that it hid Covid-19 cases from workers.
content: The judgment, which is subject to court approval, is in line with a “right to know” law that was designed to keep workers safe in the pandemic.
